framework.name: ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A Controls
framework.description: The ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A control set represented as normalized IDs, public titles, and concise implementation-oriented summaries.

controls.title: Policies for information security
controls.description: Define, approve, and communicate management-level information security policies.
controls.guidance: Maintain approved policies and review them on a scheduled basis.

controls.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Assign ownership and accountability for information security duties and decisions.
controls.guidance: Document who owns each security responsibility and keep those assignments current.

controls.title: Segregation of duties
controls.description: Separate conflicting duties so one person cannot complete high-risk actions without oversight.
controls.guidance: Split authorization, execution, and review duties for sensitive processes.

controls.title: Management responsibilities
controls.description: Require leadership to sponsor, support, and reinforce information security expectations.
controls.guidance: Make security responsibilities explicit in leadership communications and operating reviews.

controls.title: Contact with authorities
controls.description: Maintain contact channels with regulators, law enforcement, and other authorities.
controls.guidance: Define who contacts authorities and under what incident or compliance conditions.

controls.title: Contact with special interest groups
controls.description: Engage with relevant professional groups and information-sharing communities.
controls.guidance: Participate in trusted communities that improve awareness of threats and practices.

controls.title: Threat intelligence
controls.description: Collect and use threat intelligence to inform defensive decisions and priorities.
controls.guidance: Feed relevant internal and external threat information into risk and detection workflows.

controls.title: Information security in project management
controls.description: Integrate information security requirements into project planning and delivery.
controls.guidance: Capture security requirements early in each project lifecycle.

controls.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
controls.description: Maintain inventories for information, supporting assets, and their owners.
controls.guidance: Track important information assets and assign accountable owners.

controls.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
controls.description: Define rules for acceptable use of information, devices, and related assets.
controls.guidance: Publish clear usage rules and enforce them through awareness and monitoring.

controls.title: Return of assets
controls.description: Ensure organizational assets are returned when employment or engagement changes.
controls.guidance: Tie asset return checks to offboarding and role-change workflows.

controls.title: Classification of information
controls.description: Classify information according to sensitivity, value, and handling requirements.
controls.guidance: Use a defined scheme so protection requirements follow the data.

controls.title: Labelling of information
controls.description: Apply labels so people and systems can recognize handling requirements for information.
controls.guidance: Standardize labels across storage, messaging, and document workflows.

controls.title: Information transfer
controls.description: Protect information whenever it is transferred internally or externally.
controls.guidance: Use approved channels, agreements, and technical protections for information movement.

controls.title: Access control
controls.description: Establish access control policy and principles aligned to business and risk needs.
controls.guidance: Define how access is granted, reviewed, and revoked.

controls.title: Identity management
controls.description: Manage identities through provisioning, change, and deprovisioning processes.
controls.guidance: Treat identity lifecycle management as a controlled business process.

controls.title: Authentication information
controls.description: Protect passwords, tokens, keys, and other authentication information from misuse.
controls.guidance: Define secure creation, storage, delivery, and reset practices for authenticators.

controls.title: Access rights
controls.description: Grant, modify, review, and remove access rights in a controlled manner.
controls.guidance: Periodically review entitlements and remove access that no longer has a valid need.

controls.title: Information security in supplier relationships
controls.description: Address information security expectations in relationships with suppliers.
controls.guidance: Apply supplier security requirements before granting access or sharing sensitive information.

controls.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
controls.description: Embed relevant information security obligations in contracts and supplier agreements.
controls.guidance: Use standard clauses for confidentiality, audit rights, incident notice, and safeguards.

controls.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
controls.description: Manage ICT supply chain security risks across products, services, and supporting vendors.
controls.guidance: Assess supplier risk throughout selection, operation, and termination.

controls.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
controls.description: Review supplier service delivery and manage changes that could affect security.
controls.guidance: Monitor supplier performance and re-evaluate risk when services or conditions change.

controls.title: Information security for use of cloud services
controls.description: Define requirements for selecting, using, and exiting cloud services securely.
controls.guidance: Set cloud usage criteria that address data protection, identity, monitoring, and exit planning.

controls.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
controls.description: Prepare governance, roles, and procedures for information security incident handling.
controls.guidance: Define response processes, escalation paths, and preparation activities before incidents occur.

controls.title: Assessment and decision on information security events
controls.description: Evaluate security events to determine whether they require incident response.
controls.guidance: Use defined criteria to distinguish routine events from genuine incidents.

controls.title: Response to information security incidents
controls.description: Respond to information security incidents according to documented procedures.
controls.guidance: Execute response plans quickly and record key decisions and actions.

controls.title: Learning from information security incidents
controls.description: Capture lessons from incidents to improve controls, processes, and awareness.
controls.guidance: Run post-incident reviews and feed corrective actions into tracked improvement work.

controls.title: Collection of evidence
controls.description: Collect and preserve evidence so it remains reliable for investigation or legal use.
controls.guidance: Use documented evidence-handling procedures that preserve integrity and provenance.

controls.title: Information security during disruption
controls.description: Ensure security controls remain effective during crises and operational disruption.
controls.guidance: Plan how critical security safeguards will continue during degraded operations.

controls.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
controls.description: Prepare ICT capabilities so continuity objectives can be met during disruption.
controls.guidance: Align ICT resilience, recovery targets, and tests with continuity requirements.

controls.title: Identification of legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
controls.description: Identify and keep current the legal and contractual requirements relevant to information security.
controls.guidance: Maintain a register of obligations and review it when business or regulatory conditions change.

controls.title: Intellectual property rights
controls.description: Protect intellectual property and comply with related licensing and usage restrictions.
controls.guidance: Control use of protected material and evidence compliance with licensing terms.

controls.title: Protection of records
controls.description: Protect records from loss, damage, unauthorized access, and improper retention.
controls.guidance: Apply retention, storage, and disposal rules appropriate to the record type and obligation.

controls.title: Privacy and protection of PII
controls.description: Protect personally identifiable information in line with applicable privacy requirements.
controls.guidance: Map privacy obligations into data handling, access, retention, and response processes.

controls.title: Independent review of information security
controls.description: Arrange independent review to assess the adequacy and effectiveness of information security.
controls.guidance: Use periodic independent review to challenge assumptions and verify control effectiveness.

controls.title: Compliance with policies and standards for information security
controls.description: Check that information processing activities comply with internal policies and standards.
controls.guidance: Verify adherence to internal security rules with regular assessments and follow-up.

controls.title: Documented operating procedures
controls.description: Document operating procedures so information processing is performed consistently and securely.
controls.guidance: Write and maintain procedures for repeatable operational tasks and control points.

controls.title: Screening
controls.description: Screen candidates before granting them roles with access to organizational information and assets.
controls.guidance: Apply role-appropriate pre-employment checks before access is granted.

controls.title: Terms and conditions of employment
controls.description: Define information security responsibilities in employment and contractual terms.
controls.guidance: State security expectations and obligations before work begins.

controls.title: Information security awareness, education and training
controls.description: Provide awareness and training so personnel understand security responsibilities and threats.
controls.guidance: Deliver recurring role-aware training and measure whether it changes behavior.

controls.title: Disciplinary process
controls.description: Use a formal disciplinary process when information security violations occur.
controls.guidance: Apply consistent consequences when security obligations are ignored or violated.

controls.title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
controls.description: Retain and enforce security responsibilities when roles change or employment ends.
controls.guidance: Reconfirm ongoing obligations and remove access promptly during transitions.

controls.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
controls.description: Use confidentiality agreements to protect sensitive information and clarify obligations.
controls.guidance: Review confidentiality obligations so they remain aligned with current information risks.

controls.title: Remote working
controls.description: Protect information and assets when people work away from controlled premises.
controls.guidance: Define remote working controls for access, device protection, and physical privacy.

controls.title: Information security event reporting
controls.description: Require personnel to report observed security events and weaknesses quickly.
controls.guidance: Provide simple reporting channels and train people on when to use them.

controls.title: Physical security perimeter
controls.description: Define and protect the physical perimeter around sensitive facilities and areas.
controls.guidance: Use layered barriers and zoning around areas that hold sensitive assets.

controls.title: Physical entry controls
controls.description: Control and monitor who can physically enter protected areas.
controls.guidance: Use authenticated entry methods and maintain auditable visitor handling.

controls.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
controls.description: Secure offices, rooms, and facilities against unauthorized access and tampering.
controls.guidance: Apply physical safeguards appropriate to the sensitivity of each space.

controls.title: Physical security monitoring
controls.description: Monitor physical areas to detect and investigate unauthorized activity.
controls.guidance: Use physical monitoring measures that support detection and investigation.

controls.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
controls.description: Protect assets from environmental and physical threats such as fire, flood, and sabotage.
controls.guidance: Assess local hazards and implement controls that match the risk.

controls.title: Working in secure areas
controls.description: Define working rules for personnel operating in secure areas.
controls.guidance: Set practical rules for presence, observation, and activity inside secure spaces.

controls.title: Clear desk and clear screen
controls.description: Reduce accidental exposure by clearing sensitive documents and locking unattended screens.
controls.guidance: Require secure storage for sensitive material when it is not actively in use.

controls.title: Equipment siting and protection
controls.description: Place and protect equipment to reduce environmental and unauthorized access risks.
controls.guidance: Position equipment to minimize exposure to hazards and casual observation.

controls.title: Security of assets off-premises
controls.description: Protect assets when they are taken or used away from organizational premises.
controls.guidance: Apply stronger protection to portable assets that leave controlled locations.

controls.title: Storage media
controls.description: Control how storage media is handled, moved, retained, and destroyed.
controls.guidance: Track removable media and use secure handling and destruction methods.

controls.title: Supporting utilities
controls.description: Protect supporting utilities such as power, cooling, and communications needed by systems.
controls.guidance: Plan utility resilience for services that support critical information processing.

controls.title: Cabling security
controls.description: Protect power and telecommunications cabling from interception, damage, and disruption.
controls.guidance: Secure routing and access to important power and communication cables.

controls.title: Equipment maintenance
controls.description: Maintain equipment properly so it remains reliable and secure throughout its use.
controls.guidance: Control maintenance activities and preserve logs for important equipment.

controls.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
controls.description: Remove sensitive data and securely dispose of or reuse equipment.
controls.guidance: Sanitize storage media before equipment leaves organizational control.

controls.title: User endpoint devices
controls.description: Secure endpoint devices used by personnel to access information and services.
controls.guidance: Harden user devices and manage them as a controlled endpoint fleet.

controls.title: Privileged access rights
controls.description: Restrict and control privileged access rights based on clear business need.
controls.guidance: Limit elevated access and review it more frequently than standard access.

controls.title: Information access restriction
controls.description: Restrict access to information and system functions according to policy.
controls.guidance: Implement application and system controls that enforce authorized use.

controls.title: Access to source code
controls.description: Control access to source code and related repositories to protect integrity and confidentiality.
controls.guidance: Protect source code repositories with strong access control and review.

controls.title: Secure authentication
controls.description: Use strong authentication methods appropriate to the associated access risk.
controls.guidance: Adopt robust authentication and improve it for higher-risk access scenarios.

controls.title: Capacity management
controls.description: Manage system capacity so services remain available and reliable.
controls.guidance: Monitor utilization trends and plan capacity before service quality degrades.

controls.title: Protection against malware
controls.description: Prevent, detect, and recover from malware across systems and services.
controls.guidance: Use layered anti-malware controls with monitoring and response support.

controls.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
controls.description: Identify and address technical vulnerabilities in software, systems, and services.
controls.guidance: Monitor vulnerability sources and apply prioritized remediation workflows.

controls.title: Configuration management
controls.description: Define, control, and maintain secure configurations for technology assets.
controls.guidance: Standardize secure baselines and track deviations from approved configurations.

controls.title: Information deletion
controls.description: Delete information securely when it is no longer needed.
controls.guidance: Apply secure deletion methods consistent with retention and privacy needs.

controls.title: Data masking
controls.description: Mask or transform sensitive data to reduce unnecessary exposure.
controls.guidance: Use masking or anonymization when full sensitive values are not required.

controls.title: Data leakage prevention
controls.description: Detect and prevent unauthorized disclosure or exfiltration of sensitive data.
controls.guidance: Deploy controls that detect risky transfers and block or alert on them.

controls.title: Information backup
controls.description: Create, protect, and test backups needed to restore information and services.
controls.guidance: Back up critical information, protect the backups, and test restoration routinely.

controls.title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
controls.description: Use redundancy to reduce single points of failure in information processing facilities.
controls.guidance: Design redundancy for services whose failure would materially affect operations.

controls.title: Logging
controls.description: Generate and retain logs that support monitoring, analysis, and investigation.
controls.guidance: Capture security-relevant events with enough context to support investigation.

controls.title: Monitoring activities
controls.description: Monitor systems and services to detect abnormal or unauthorized activity.
controls.guidance: Monitor important assets continuously and route alerts into an actionable process.

controls.title: Clock synchronisation
controls.description: Synchronize system clocks so logs and time-dependent functions remain trustworthy.
controls.guidance: Use trusted time sources and verify clock consistency across important systems.

controls.title: Use of privileged utility programs
controls.description: Control utility programs that can override or bypass normal application and system controls.
controls.guidance: Restrict use of powerful system tools and monitor who runs them.

controls.title: Installation of software on operational systems
controls.description: Control software installation on production systems to reduce unauthorized change and risk.
controls.guidance: Require approval and validation before software is introduced into operational systems.

controls.title: Network security
controls.description: Manage and protect networks so information systems and data remain secure in transit.
controls.guidance: Protect networks with controlled architecture, filtering, and secure protocols.

controls.title: Security of network services
controls.description: Define and manage the security requirements of network services in use.
controls.guidance: Set security expectations for internal and external network services.

controls.title: Segregation of networks
controls.description: Separate networks and services to reduce exposure and limit attack spread.
controls.guidance: Use logical or physical separation to isolate systems with different trust levels.

controls.title: Web filtering
controls.description: Manage access to web resources to reduce exposure to malicious or inappropriate content.
controls.guidance: Filter unsafe web destinations and align browsing controls to risk.

controls.title: Use of cryptography
controls.description: Define and apply cryptographic protections appropriate to business and legal needs.
controls.guidance: Specify where cryptography is required and how keys are governed.

controls.title: Secure development life cycle
controls.description: Integrate security activities throughout the software development life cycle.
controls.guidance: Embed security requirements, reviews, and gates across development and release.

controls.title: Application security requirements
controls.description: Define security requirements for applications before building, acquiring, or changing them.
controls.guidance: Capture security needs before development or procurement work starts.

controls.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
controls.description: Apply secure architecture and engineering principles to systems and supporting technologies.
controls.guidance: Use documented architecture principles to guide system and solution design.

controls.title: Secure coding
controls.description: Develop software using secure coding practices that reduce common vulnerabilities.
controls.guidance: Define coding standards and verify adherence with review and testing.

controls.title: Security testing in development and acceptance
controls.description: Perform security testing during development and before release or acceptance.
controls.guidance: Include security tests in build, test, and release readiness criteria.

controls.title: Outsourced development
controls.description: Manage and oversee outsourced development so external work meets security expectations.
controls.guidance: Set security requirements and review mechanisms for externally developed software.

controls.title: Separation of development, test and production environments
controls.description: Separate development, test, and production environments to reduce operational and data exposure risk.
controls.guidance: Prevent uncontrolled mixing of code, access, and live data across environments.

controls.title: Change management
controls.description: Manage changes to systems and services through formal assessment, approval, and tracking.
controls.guidance: Require controlled change workflows for security-relevant technology changes.

controls.title: Test information
controls.description: Protect test information so it does not expose sensitive or production data unnecessarily.
controls.guidance: Use sanitized or controlled test information whenever realistic data is needed.

controls.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
controls.description: Plan audit and test activity so it does not undermine the security or availability of systems.
controls.guidance: Coordinate audit activity carefully and limit its operational impact.
